Motion To Compel Depositions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-26-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
JONATHAN JOINER, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFFS/MOVING PARTIES, BETTY AND FRANK D'EREDITA
EUNICE CHANG, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, MONIQUE ETCHEVERRY
RULING: MATTER ARGUED. PLAINTIFFS BETTY D'EREDITA, FRANK D'EREDITA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS AND FOR SANCTIONS. Motion is granted, BUT THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. There was no basis for defendant not to produce expert witnesses for deposition at the dates and times indicated. Defendant's written objections to each of the notices of taking expert deposition are not well taken: CCP 2034.410 does not require plaintiff to issue a subpoena. The time requirements of CCP 2025.270(a) are met here (plaintiff gave more than 10 days notice), and the references in the remainder of CCP 2025.270 regarding CCP 1985.3 are not applicable. CCP 2020(d)(3), which is now renumbered as CCP 2020.420, is inapplicable. CCP 2020.410(c) is not applicable, nor are its time requirements applicable. CCP 2034.410( c ) does not require a subpoena to compel a retained expert to produce records and files. See CCP 2034.210, et. seq. regarding demand for exchange of expert witness information, which the court presumes was exchanged here. Nor is personal service of a subpoena on an expert required. See CCP 2034.460(a). Defendant shall make its expert witnesses available as early as possible after the hearing in this matter so that the depositions can be concluded by no later than August 1, 2005. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S EXPERT WITNESSES SHALL PRODUCE AT DEPOSITION ALL DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY DEMANDS FOR EXCHANGE OF EXPERT WITNESS INFORMATION, WHICH THE PARTIES INDICATED WERE SERVED ON ONE ANOTHER PURSUANT TO CCP 2034.210 ET SEQ. (622/M. MILLER)
